features.summary: Nike reported Q2 FY25 revenue down 8% (9% currency-neutral) to meet expectations amid ongoing franchise management headwinds. New CEO Elliott Hill outlined strategic repositioning focused on returning sport to the center of Nike's identity through sport-led 'fields of play' teams, reducing reliance on classic footwear franchises, and rebuilding wholesale partnerships. The company is accelerating inventory liquidation and shifting Nike Direct from promotional to premium full-price positioning, which will pressure near-term results but aims to restore marketplace health.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued revenue declines through fiscal 2025 as they execute strategic reset actions. Q3 revenue guidance is down low double-digits with gross margins declining 300-350 basis points due to inventory liquidation and marketplace repositioning. Q4 headwinds are expected to be greater than Q3 across revenue, margins, and expenses. The company is investing in demand creation, sports marketing partnerships (NFL, NBA, WNBA), and key city/country teams while reducing performance marketing spend in favor of brand marketing.
features.risks: Near-term financial performance will be pressured by inventory liquidation, wholesale discounts, higher factory store promotions, and reduced promotional activity on Nike Direct causing traffic declines. Greater China faces challenging macro conditions and competitive pressures requiring higher markdown activity. The transition from push to pull marketplace model creates execution risk, and rebuilding wholesale relationships requires earning back shelf space and open-to-buy dollars from partners.
features.key_drivers: Success depends on sport-focused product innovation through new 'fields of play' organizational structure covering running, basketball, training, football, and sportswear. Key growth drivers include reducing classic footwear franchise concentration, launching new seasonal products for fall/holiday 2025, and rebuilding authentic brand marketing around sports moments. Marketplace health restoration through premium Nike Direct positioning and strengthened wholesale partnerships is critical for sustainable growth recovery.
